race.name: Sjusjoen Skimarathon
race.tagline: A full marathon on one of Norway's finest trail networks — the gateway to Birkebeinerrennet qualification.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple aid stations along the course

climate.description: Sjusjoen sits at 800-900m ASL in Innlandet, providing reliable winter conditions from December through April. Race day temperatures typically range from -15°C to -3°C in the January-February timeframe.
challenges: Variable conditions across the extensive trail network
Wind exposure on open mountain sections
Wax selection critical at mountain altitude

course.primary: A 42km marathon-distance course on the extensive Sjusjoen trail network at 800-900m altitude. Light, varied terrain through one of Norway's premier cross-country skiing areas.
course.surface: Machine-groomed tracks on Norway's premier XC ski network
course.grooming: Excellent — Sjusjoen is one of Norway's best-groomed trail networks
features: One of Norway's premier XC skiing destinations
Extensive trail network with excellent grooming
Seeding race for Birkebeinerrennet and Vasaloppet
Light, varied terrain at mountain altitude

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Strong infrastructure on one of Norway's best trail networks with excellent snow reliability and grooming. Primarily valued as a Birkebeinerrennet and Vasaloppet seeding race. Solid participation for a regional event.

history.summary: The Sjusjoen Skimarathon emerged in the 1990s on one of Norway's most extensive and well-maintained cross-country skiing trail networks. Sjusjoen has been a premier Norwegian skiing destination for decades, and the marathon leverages its world-class infrastructure. The race serves as an important seeding event for both Birkebeinerrennet and Vasaloppet.
notable_facts: Located on one of Norway's premier XC skiing trail networks
Seeding race for both Birkebeinerrennet and Vasaloppet
Both classic and freestyle technique options
Sjusjoen offers some of the best-groomed trails in Scandinavia
Situated at 800-900m altitude for reliable snow conditions

youtube_data.search_query: Sjusjoen Skimarathon cross country ski race Sjusjoen

videos.title: The Cross Country Skiing Paradise Of Norway
videos.description:
Cross country skiing, Norway’s national sport, and where better place to nordic ski than arguably Norway’s best cross country skiing location: Sjusjøen. 

Also known as Nordic skiing, sometimes ski touring, or simply XC skiing, this sport is growing increasingly popular thanks to the combination of exercise, technique, and often stunning scenery. 

360km of trails makes Sjusjøen a top Nordic skiing destination, with people travelling from all over Norway, and even the world, to ski here. Are the boys becoming the new johannes klæbo? Will they maybe Even meet Øystein Pettersen? 

Sjusjøen is the location for several cross country skiing races throughout the ski season, including the famous Birkebeinerrennet which runs through Sjusjøen.  


Cross country skiing, or langrennsski as it’s known in Norwegian, actually has two quite distinct styles, classic and skating (klassisk and skøyte in Norwegian respectively). The equipment, and more importantly technique, are quite different in each. Most XC skiing beginners start with classic, and then move onto skating which is usually considered more challenging. 

Many professionals come to Sjusjøen to train, including Petter Northug, Johannes Klæbo, Marit Bjørgen, Øystein Pettersen, many of whom have won Olympic medals and represent the best of the best at the sport. And not to forget Therese Johaug. 

Nordic skiing can be a serious workout and many consider skiers to have some of the highest VO2 max levels of any support. However, it’s also a popular activity with recreational skiers who simply enjoy heading out for a leisurely trip.

Should we make a episode with Therese Johaug? 

For you collaborations please do contact us on Thomas@becomenorwegian.com


#crosscountryskiing #xcskiing #crosscountryski

videos.curation_reason: Recent skier-filmed POV content showcasing Sjusjøen terrain with detailed commentary on skiing techniques and conditions.

videos.title: Ljøsheimrunden - Sjusjøen
videos.description:
Starter på Sjusjøen Langrennsarena. Passer i alt type vær. Passerer Midtjfellkoia hvor det er servering i helger gjennom vinteren. Mulighet for pilking Ljøsvatnet. Servering på Hygga fjellkro.


www.visitsjusjoen.no
Destinasjon Sjusjøen - et eldorado for aktiviteter på ski, sykkel og sti - vi setter spor!

videos.title: Lillehammer Trollski Marathon 2018 - Glimt fra Rennet Venabygdsfjellet - Sjusjøen
videos.description: Årets renn gikk i strålende solskinn og ikke et vindpust på fjellet. Årets renn hadde  nærmere 800 deltagere i alt fra den lengste konkurranseklassen på 120 kilometer til korteste trim og familieklasse på 13 kilometer.

videos.title: Lunkefjellet - Sjusjøen
videos.description:
Starter på Sjusjøen Langrennsarena og etter kort tid passerer dere Matstasjonen, hvor det er åpent for servering i helger gjennom vinteren. Det er en fantastisk tur når det er solskinn og fint vær. På uværsdager blir det ikke kjørt opp her. På toppen av Lunkefjellet så er utsikten svært god vestover mot Jotunheimen og Rondane



www.visitsjusjoen.no
Destinasjon Sjusjøen - et eldorado for aktiviteter på ski, sykkel og sti - vi setter spor!

videos.title: Lillehammer Troll Ski Marathon 2018 - Venabygdsfjellet - Sjusjøen
videos.description: De første som startet på den 95 kilometer lange løypa dro fra Venabygdsfjellet klokken 07:00 på morgenen. Utover dagen ble de etterfulgt av trim- og konkurranseløpere på alle 4 distanser der de siste var i mål nærmere 19:00 på kvelden. Mange fikk en lang dag i fjellet med strålende vår, skyfri himmel og realt påskeføre utover dagen.
videos.curation_reason: Official race coverage showing marathon event atmosphere and conditions at Sjusjøen.

quotes.text: 360 km of pristine groomed cross-country skiing slopes, everyone we talk to they say this is the place to be, this is the cross country paradise of Norway. Snow comes quite early and it stays the whole season, and it's actually one of the most snow sure places in Norway as well.

A lot of flat terrain like this which is perfect for beginners like us who are looking to learn, but it's also where all the pros and the Olympians come as well - you got some really steep incline and some really challenging terrain so there's something for everyone.
